title: rubus vulgatus
intro: Rubus vulgatus, also known as the wild raspberry or bramble, is a type of flowering shrub that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Rubus vulgatus include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and aid in digestion, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Rubus vulgatus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be used topically as a salve or ointment to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
